Tools
Production & Pricing Tools
Caramel Colour Calculator
Calculate E150a caramel additions for colour adjustment. SOP 5.05.10 compliant with print record.
Live
Product Pricing Tool
Build detailed pricing quotes including production costs, duties, margins, and client terms.
Live
ABV & Dilution Calculator
Calculate water addition volumes for precise ABV targeting. Supports temperature correction and Anton-Par validation.
Coming Soon
Liquid Ratio Tool
Model blending ratios across multiple parcels. Calculates weighted ABV, age statements, and volume outputs.
Coming Soon
Duty & Tax Calculator
Irish Revenue excise duty, VAT, and margin calculations. Supports 700ml, 500ml, and 200ml formats.
Coming Soon
Label Compliance Checker
Validate Irish Whiskey label requirements against Technical File specifications and Revenue rules.
Planned
Batch Yield Analyser
Track actual vs expected yields across filling runs. Flags loss-of-account variances for excise reporting.
Planned
Production Scheduler
Plan and schedule batch runs, filling dates, and client delivery milestones in one view.
Planned
Client Quote Builder
Generate branded PDF quotations for private label clients with full cost breakdowns and T&Cs.
Planned

Calculator
Lighthouse Drinks
5.05.10 — Caramel Colouring Tool E150a Addition Calculator · SOP Reference
Irish Whiskey · Private Label

SOP 5.05.10

Caramel Colour Addition Calculator

1 Batch Details
L
2 Colour Specification
AU
Enter a value above
AU
Enter a value above
AU
Measured from lab trial (§6.3). Default 0.020 if unknown.
§6.3 Calculation Method (SOP)
Lab Trial: Add caramel dropwise to 100 mL whiskey sample until target colour reached.
Formula: Caramel Required (L) = [Trial mL Added / 100] × Batch Volume (L)
Colour Delta: ΔAU = Target − Current absorbance at 430 nm
3 SOP Procedure Reference
6.1
Pre-Addition Checks
Verify whiskey in IBC, volume, and target colour spec. Confirm E150a grade & expiry date. Ensure caramel at room temperature. Calibrate dosing equipment (500g weights available).
6.2
Caramel Preparation
Shake/stir container before use. If viscosity high, warm to 30–40°C max (indirect heating only). Record caramel density if required.
6.3
Lab Trial & Calculation
Take 100 mL whiskey sample. Add caramel dropwise to achieve target colour. Record mL added — use above calculator to scale to full batch. Cross-verify with colour specification limits.
6.4
Caramel Addition
Start gentle agitation in tank/IBC. Add caramel slowly near agitator vortex. Mix for 20–30 minutes. Take initial sample, visually check colour. Adjust in increments ≤10% of initial dose.
6.5
Post-Addition Mixing
Continue mixing 15 minutes for homogeneity. Rest whiskey 30–60 minutes for colour stabilisation. Collect samples for testing.
7
Quality Control
Visual colour check vs. approved batch OR spectrophotometer at 430 nm. ABV via Anton-Par — caramel drop should be <0.05%. Conduct 24-hour stability check: no sediment, no colour separation, no haze.
9
Documentation Required
Complete 5.01.03 Caramel Colouring Record v003 and 5.04.02 ABV Calibration checks & Result. All liquid must be signed off by Liquid Manager & Quality Specialist before bottling.
⚗ Calculation Result
🏮 Enter batch volume, current colour, and target colour, then calculate.
Caramel to Add (E150a)
—
Litres
Colour Scale (430nm Absorbance)
Current
Target

Colour Delta (ΔAU) —
Caramel (mL) —
Caramel (g approx.) —
Max Increment (≤10%) —
Est. ABV Impact —
ABV Note: Caramel is non-alcoholic. Expected ABV drop is <0.05% — within acceptable limits per §7.2.
Please check your inputs — target colour must be higher than current colour.
Pre-Addition Checklist (§6.1)

Compliance Declaration
This batch was prepared in full accordance with SOP 5.05.10 Caramel Colouring Procedure. All pre-addition checks, safety precautions, dosing, mixing, and post-addition procedures were completed as per the approved procedure. Quality control checks including visual colour verification and ABV measurement have been carried out. Documentation records 5.01.03 and 5.04.02 have been completed.
